title: 20th-Anniversary folk-art poster screen-print separation pack — approved master vectorized, white underbase + 4 spot-colour halftone plates with dot-gain choke, a grain-textured proof, the authored registration sheet rendered out of Illustrator, and a campus-reference Firefly board
grounding_note: VERBATIM from the cand_52 Upwork brief: the client is Bayou Cross Classical Academy, a Catholic classical school in Lafayette, Louisiana; the project is a commemorative 20th-Anniversary poster; the wanted aesthetic is explicitly "festival posters, folk-art illustration, screenprint aesthetics, and narrative poster design"; the artwork must "feel handcrafted, meaningful, celebratory, and rich with discoverable details," weaving faith, academics, student life, community traditions, and campus landmarks into one cohesive composition; the brief package includes a creative brief, campus maps, campus reference photos, typography guidance, and deliverables/timeline. ENRICHED to make it a runnable Adobe-connector benchmark: the original ask (drawing the story-driven illustration from scratch) is bespoke hand-illustration, which the connector cannot do (it is on the capability sheet's "no" list — no text-to-image / from-scratch art). So this task is faithfully scoped to the screenprint_seps PRODUCTION phase that genuinely follows approval of that artwork: the client supplies the APPROVED, finalized folk-art master (the agreed illustration), the campus reference photos named in the brief, and a print shop's authored Illustrator separation/registration sheet (.ai). The freelancer's Adobe job is to turn that approved master into a real spot-colour screen-print separation pack — exactly the "screenprint aesthetics / screenprint" deliverable the brief calls out. Spot-colour names (white underbase + four screen-print inks) and the 24x36 in @300dpi poster trim are reasonable, standard screen-print enrichments consistent with a commemorative wall poster; nothing contradicts the brief. No campus name, denomination, location, anniversary number, or aesthetic was invented — all are verbatim.
one_line_ask: From the approved folk-art 20th-Anniversary master, produce a print-ready 5-screen separation pack (white underbase + four spot-colour halftone plates with a dot-gain choke), a vectorized hard-edge lettering/line plate, a grain-textured client proof, the registration/separation sheet rendered out of the supplied Illustrator template (PDF, PNG and SVG as three separate render passes), and a Firefly board of the campus reference photos for the screen printer.
full_brief: Bayou Cross Classical Academy (a classical Christian school in the fictional town of Sainte-Colombe, Louisiana) has APPROVED the final folk-art / festival-poster illustration celebrating the school's first twenty years — a handcrafted, story-driven composition weaving faith, academics, student life, community traditions, and campus landmarks, rich with discoverable details. The illustration phase is complete and signed off; this engagement is the screen-print PRODUCTION phase. You are given: (1) the approved master artwork as a high-resolution flattened RGB PNG (~24x36 in @300dpi, full-bleed), in which the figurative folk-art scene carries soft tonal passages and the commemorative title/year lettering is hard-edge solid; (2) the campus reference photos that accompanied the creative brief (chapel, oak quad, schoolhouse facade, founding-class photo); and (3) the print shop's authored Illustrator separation/registration sheet template (separations.ai) carrying real artboards, registration marks, and ink-name slots.\n\nDeliver a 5-screen spot-colour separation pack for a manual screen print on natural/cream stock: a WHITE UNDERBASE plate (solid fill behind all art), and four spot-ink plates — Marian Blue, Antique Gold, Brick Red, and Ink Black — where the continuous-tone folk-art passages are converted to a registered Ben-Day halftone dot field and CHOKED (a light blur pass on the dot layer tightens the dot edges) so dots do not plug on press, while the title lettering and key line-work stay hard-edge solid via a vectorized hold-out plate. Because image_vectorize traces a whole raster and has no mask input, first knock the figurative art field out to flat with a solid fill (leaving only the masked lettering/line-work on the canvas), then vectorize that lettering-only raster to the crisp hold-out SVG. Provide a single flattened grain-textured client proof that previews the screen-print \"tooth\" on cream stock, the registration/separation sheet rendered out of the supplied Illustrator template as three separate export passes (PDF, PNG and SVG — document_render_vector emits one media type per call), and a Firefly Boards moodboard collecting the campus reference photos so the printer can match landmark colours. Every separation must share identical trim and registration so the five screens line up on the press.

inputs.name: approved_master_poster.png
inputs.gen_prompt: A handcrafted folk-art / festival-poster style illustration celebrating a classical Christian school's 20th anniversary, portrait orientation roughly 2:3, on a cream paper ground. A dense narrative composition weaving small vignettes: a chapel with a cross, students in uniform reading books, an oak-lined quad, a schoolhouse facade, a choir, families at a community feast, scrolls and laurels, a banner reading 'BAYOU CROSS CLASSICAL ACADEMY' across the top and '20 YEARS - 2006-2026' along the bottom in clean serif lettering. Limited screen-print palette of deep Marian blue, antique gold, brick red and ink black on cream. Soft flat tonal shading inside the figures, hard-edge solid lettering. Slight hand-printed registration imperfection, visible paper texture, individual line-work strokes — looks like a real handmade screen-print poster, never CGI-glossy. Flattened, full-bleed, high resolution.
inputs.realism_notes: gemini-3-pro-image chosen because the deliverable hinges on legible, correctly-spelled commemorative TITLE and YEAR lettering plus a logo-like banner — its strength. Must look like a finalized, approved flat illustration (the client's sign-off artwork), with genuine paper tooth and limited spot-ink palette so the halftone/separation steps have real soft-tone passages AND hard-edge lettering to separate. No photographic depth, no gradients deeper than flat screen-print shading. Client and banner text are the fictional 'Bayou Cross Classical Academy' — no real school, denomination-figure, or person is depicted.

inputs.name: campus_chapel.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: A candid documentary photo of a small Christian school chapel exterior in coastal Louisiana — warm brick and white trim, a simple cross above the door, live oaks framing the entrance, soft Gulf-coast afternoon light. Real architectural detail, natural shadows, slight lens imperfection, no people. Reference-photo look, not a render.
inputs.realism_notes: gpt-image-2 for photoreal architecture. One of four campus reference photos named in the brief; used only inside the Firefly reference board, not edited into the artwork. Generic fictional campus, no identifiable real building.

inputs.name: campus_oak_quad.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: A candid documentary photo of a school courtyard quad lined with large Southern live oaks draped in Spanish moss, brick walkways, a few uniformed students walking in the distance, dappled afternoon light. Natural colours, real depth of field, authentic campus-snapshot feel.
inputs.realism_notes: gpt-image-2 photoreal. Campus landmark colour reference for the printer's Firefly board.

inputs.name: campus_schoolhouse.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: A candid documentary photo of a modest classical schoolhouse facade — warm brick, white columns, a dated 2006 cornerstone plaque beside the entrance, natural daylight, real texture and minor wear. Authentic reference-photo look, no people.
inputs.realism_notes: gpt-image-2 photoreal. The schoolhouse-facade reference named in the brief; Firefly board only. Generic anonymous campus building, no identifiable real place.

inputs.name: campus_foundingclass.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: A candid documentary photo of a small framed founding-class group portrait mounted on a schoolhouse interior wall — a modest sepia/colour class photo behind glass with a '2006' brass plate, natural daylight, real reflections and minor wear. Authentic archival/reference photo look.
inputs.realism_notes: gpt-image-2 photoreal. The founding-class reference named in the brief; Firefly board only. Generic anonymous group, no real named people.

inputs.name: separations.ai
inputs.gen_prompt: Generate a realistic Adobe Illustrator separation/registration sheet template description and accompanying .ai-equivalent SVG/structure: a single document with five labelled spot-colour artboards (WHITE UNDERBASE, MARIAN BLUE, ANTIQUE GOLD, BRICK RED, INK BLACK), crop/registration marks at all four corners and centre, an ink-name + mesh-count text slot under each artboard, and a 24x36in trim with 0.125in bleed guides. Saved as a genuine .ai (vector) the connector can render.
inputs.realism_notes: AUTHORED .ai is a hard requirement: document_render_vector only EXPORTS a genuine .ai (it does not compose layout), and emits ONE media type per call. This is the print shop's standard registration sheet, supplied by the client/printer — exactly how the reference screen-print tasks treat the authored vector template. gemini-2.5-flash generates the structured vector/template scaffold.

outputs.name: plate_white_underbase.png
outputs.spec: White underbase separation, 24x36in @300dpi, solid fill behind all printable art on transparency — first screen down on cream stock.

outputs.name: plate_marian_blue_halftone.png
outputs.spec: Marian Blue spot plate: Ben-Day halftone dot field over the tonal folk-art passages, dot-gain choked, lettering held out solid; trim/registration identical to all plates.

outputs.name: plate_antique_gold_halftone.png
outputs.spec: Antique Gold spot plate, same registered halftone geometry, choked, on its colour build.

outputs.name: plate_brick_red_halftone.png
outputs.spec: Brick Red spot plate, same registered halftone geometry, choked.

outputs.name: plate_ink_black_lettering.png
outputs.spec: Ink Black plate carrying the vectorized hard-edge title/year lettering and key line-work as solid (no dots) plus the darkest tonal dots.

outputs.name: lettering_lineart.svg
outputs.spec: Vectorized clean SVG of the hard-edge commemorative lettering and key line-work — traced from a lettering-only raster (art field knocked flat by a solid fill first, since image_vectorize has no mask input); the hold-out master that keeps type crisp across screens and scales to wall size.

outputs.name: client_proof_grain.png
outputs.spec: Single flattened client proof of the assembled look with screen-print grain/tooth on cream stock, for sign-off before burning screens.

outputs.name: separation_registration_sheet.pdf
outputs.spec: Registration/separation sheet rendered from the authored separations.ai (all five labelled artboards with crop/reg marks + ink names) to print-ready PDF. Matching PNG and SVG exports are produced as two additional document_render_vector passes (one media type per call).

outputs.name: separation_registration_sheet.png
outputs.spec: Registration/separation sheet rasterized to PNG (all five labelled artboards with crop/registration marks + ink names), rendered from separations.ai at step 20 — a screen-viewable proof of the plate layout for quick email/preview sharing alongside the print PDF.

outputs.name: separation_registration_sheet.svg
outputs.spec: Registration/separation sheet exported to editable SVG (vector, same five labelled artboards with crop/registration marks + ink names), rendered from separations.ai at step 21 — hands the print shop a scalable, editable copy of the separation layout.

outputs.name: campus_reference_board
outputs.spec: Firefly Boards deep-link moodboard collecting the four campus reference photos so the printer can colour-match landmarks (chapel brick, oak greens, gold).

connector_workflow.note: Open an upload session for the client drop: approved_master_poster.png, the four campus reference photos, and the authored separations.ai registration template.
Finalize uploads; returns stable presigned URLs (master, campus photos, separations.ai) that seed every downstream chain.
Inline-preview the approved master to confirm it landed intact and to read the live print extent / lettering vs tonal regions before separating (inspect only).
Trim to the true live print area so screen budget and registration use the real extent — the shared canvas every plate forks from.
Normalize overall tone of the approved master so blacks read true and the spot-ink builds separate cleanly — the master raster all five plates derive from.
Mask 'the title and year lettering and banner text' to isolate the hard-edge type/line-work that must stay solid (never dotted).
Invert the lettering mask to get the figurative folk-art field — the region that becomes the halftone dot field (lettering held out).

Solid flat fill of the art-field region on the master so only the masked lettering/line-work remains on the canvas — a lettering-only raster to vectorize (image_vectorize has no mask input, so the field must be knocked out first). Solid-fill within the existing canvas; no growth/gen-fill.
Trace the lettering-only raster (single imageURI, no mask) to a clean SVG hold-out plate — crisp type that scales to wall size and prints as solid on the Ink Black screen.
Ben-Day halftone (blackAndWhite, registered dot geometry) INSIDE the art-field mask only — the shared screen-print dot field all four spot plates share.
Dot-gain CHOKE — a light gaussian pass on the dot layer tightens dot edges so they don't plug shadows on absorbent cream stock — the press-safe master dot field.
Select all printable art off the cream ground; invert-ready mask whose inverse is the underbase footprint.
Solid white fill (preset:white) of the art footprint -> WHITE UNDERBASE plate, the first screen laid down under the colours on cream stock. Solid-fill within the existing canvas.
Tint the choked dot field to the Marian Blue spot build -> MARIAN BLUE plate proof (registration identical to all plates).
Re-key the same registered dot field to the Antique Gold spot build -> ANTIQUE GOLD plate (registration identical to all plates).
Tint the same registered choked dot field to the Brick Red spot build -> BRICK RED plate (same halftone geometry and registration as all plates).

Build the INK BLACK plate: key the choked dot field to the ink-black spot build AND overlay the vectorized hard-edge lettering/line-work from lettering_lineart.svg as solid (no dots) on the same trim/registration -> the darkest tonal dots plus the crisp solid type/line-work on one screen.
Add screen-print grain/tooth to the assembled choked field for a flattened cream-stock CLIENT PROOF to sign off before screens are burned.

Render the authored separations.ai (five labelled spot artboards + crop/reg marks + ink-name slots) to print-ready PDF (outputMediaType=pdf). Needs the genuine .ai input; render only exports it, one media type per call.
Second render pass of the same separations.ai to PNG (outputMediaType=png) — separate call because render_vector emits one media type per call.
Third render pass of the same separations.ai to SVG (outputMediaType=svg) for the printer's scalable copy.

Assemble the four uploaded campus reference photos (chapel, oak quad, schoolhouse, founding class) into a Firefly Boards moodboard deep-link so the screen printer can colour-match the chapel brick, oak greens, and gold.

chaining_note: Strict output-to-input chain. Steps 1-2 load all client assets (upload_session -> finalized_assets). Step 3 previews the master (finalized_assets -> previewed_master, inspect only). The raster spine then flows linearly: previewed_master -> cropped_master (4) -> normalized_master (5), which is the single master raster every plate forks from. Step 6 derives lettering_mask from normalized_master; step 7 inverts it -> art_field_mask; step 8 solid-fills the art field on normalized_master (using art_field_mask) to leave a lettering-only raster; step 9 vectorizes that lettering-only raster (single imageURI, no mask, since image_vectorize has no mask input) -> lettering_lineart.svg. Step 10 consumes normalized_master + art_field_mask -> halftone_field; step 11 chokes it -> choked_field, the shared dot master. Step 12 derives art_subject_mask from cropped_master; step 13 consumes choked_field + art_subject_mask -> white underbase plate. Steps 14, 15, 16 each consume choked_field -> the Marian Blue, Antique Gold and Brick Red spot plates (same registered geometry). Step 17 builds the Ink Black plate from choked_field PLUS the vectorized lettering_lineart.svg (step 9) so the darkest tonal dots and the solid hard-edge type/line-work share one screen. Step 18 consumes choked_field -> grain proof. Steps 19, 20, 21 each consume the authored separations.ai (from finalized_assets) and render it to PDF, PNG and SVG respectively — three separate document_render_vector calls because the tool emits one media type per call. Step 22 consumes the four campus photos (from finalized_assets) -> Firefly board. Every step after the initial load reads a prior step's named output; the 5-screen separation pack, vector lettering plate, proof, registration sheet (three formats), and reference board all emerge from the chain.
difficulty_rationale: Long-horizon and wide-mix: 22 connector calls, all server="adobe", 17 distinct Adobe tools across the asset, image, and document groups — well beyond the >=10 calls / >=6 distinct-tools bar. It is a genuine screenprint_seps production job mirroring two already-validated reference tasks (the 4-colour screen-print separation pack and the VOIDRUNNER variant covers): masked halftone dot field, dot-gain choke, five spot-colour plates each produced by an explicit step from one shared choked master, a vectorized hard-edge hold-out that also composites into the Ink Black screen, a grain proof, and an authored-.ai registration sheet exported via render_vector. Difficulty comes from correct mask polarity (lettering held out of the dot field; subject-inverse drives the underbase), keeping all five separations on identical trim/registration, and respecting tool limits: image_fill_area is solid-fill only (white underbase, not generative), document_render_vector only EXPORTS the genuine separations.ai, create_firefly_board only assembles existing assets, and no step uses generative fill, object removal, compositing-from-scratch, text-to-image, or upscaling. The original brief's from-scratch folk-art illustration is intentionally out of connector scope and is supplied as the approved client master, keeping the task honest and fully [C] headless-runnable.

reverify.banned_capability_check: No banned capability. The from-scratch folk-art illustration is supplied as an approved client input (approved_master_poster.png), never generated. image_fill_area is used only for solid/flat fills WITHIN the existing canvas (knocking the art field flat at step 8, solid white preset underbase at step 13) — never to grow, square, or extend the canvas. image_vectorize traces an existing raster with a single imageURI (no mask param), fed a lettering-only raster prepared by the prior fill step. image_apply_gaussian_blur is a whole-image blur on the dot layer (dot-gain choke). document_render_vector only EXPORTS the genuine authored separations.ai (three passes, one media type each). create_firefly_board only assembles already-uploaded assets. No image_crop_and_resize/reframe-crop, no upscale/super-resolution, no text-to-image, no from-scratch art/logo, no object-removal-with-reconstruction, no mask fed to image_apply_lens_blur, no asset_preview 'previewing into a new file'.
reverify.notes: All 22 steps are server=adobe, exec_mode=C (headless), fully output->input chained. 17 distinct Adobe tools across asset/image/document groups (>=6 bar) and 22 calls (>=10 bar). Each of the five separation plates now has its own explicit numbered step (white underbase 13, Marian Blue 14, Antique Gold 15, Brick Red 16, Ink Black 17); the Ink Black step composites the choked dot field with the vectorized lettering_lineart.svg so the plate carries dots plus solid type as its output spec requires. The four campus reference photos are four distinct input files (chapel, oak quad, schoolhouse, founding class) matching every 'four' reference in the brief, the board output, and step 22. IP fixed: the real institution 'Bayou Cross Academy' (Lafayette, LA — real school invoking Pope John Paul II) is replaced with the invented 'Bayou Cross Classical Academy' in the fictional town of Sainte-Colombe, LA, with the papal-figure reference dropped; both mappings are in brand_rename. Adobe/Firefly/Upwork left unchanged as allowed platforms. Stale text reconciled: no field still claims a mask-driven vectorize or a single-call PDF+PNG+SVG render.
